items.title: Looking At The Narrative For UnitedHealth Group UNH After Shifting Earnings Recovery Expectations
items.description: UnitedHealth Group’s fair value estimate has been nudged up from about US$389 to roughly US$392 per share, as recent Street research has turned somewhat more constructive on the company’s long term earnings recovery story. A stable discount rate around 7.0% and a slightly firmer revenue growth outlook, supported by views on Medicare Advantage, Medicaid margin normalization, and Optum restructuring progress, are helping to anchor this updated pricing narrative. Stay tuned to see how you can...

items.title: Is UnitedHealth Group (UNH) Attractive After A 33% One Year Share Price Decline
items.description: If you have been wondering whether UnitedHealth Group shares still offer fair value after recent volatility, this breakdown will help you frame the trade off between price and quality. The stock last closed at US$336.40, with returns of 2.3% over the past 7 days and 1.7% over the last 30 days, alongside a 32.9% decline over 1 year and a 27.5% decline over 3 years. Recent headlines around UnitedHealth Group have focused on the stock's longer term pullback and what that means for investor...

items.title: Biotech and Pharma Stocks Tumble as Investors Race into Energy
items.description: Cash has flowed into oil stocks following the U.S.’s seizing of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro.

items.title: Berkshire Hathaway dethrones Allianz as world’s largest insurer
items.description: Investing.com -- Berkshire Hathaway has claimed the top spot in AM Best’s ranking of the world’s 25 largest insurance companies by non-banking assets, surpassing Allianz SE, the global rating agency announced on Monday.
